description_long: This object is a rectangular, elevated planter box made of dark wood, supported by four sturdy metal legs. The planter is filled with a layer of light-colored gravel or soil, from which several green, lanceolate-leaved plants emerge. The plants feature multiple yellow flowers with dark centers, each flower displaying a radial arrangement of petals. The flowers are distributed across the length of the planter, creating a natural, slightly wild appearance. The overall construction suggests durability and suitability for outdoor urban environments such as sidewalks, parks, or plazas. The combination of natural and artificial materials gives the object both functional and decorative value.
description: A dark wooden rectangular planter box on metal legs, filled with gravel and green plants bearing yellow, daisy-like flowers. The flowers are radially arranged and distributed along the length of the planter.
description_view_0: Front view shows the narrow end of the planter with a dense cluster of yellow flowers and green leaves emerging from the gravel.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the full length of the planter, displaying the spread of flowers and foliage along the top surface.
description_view_2: Opposite end view highlights another narrow side, with a similar dense cluster of flowers and leaves as the front.
description_view_3: Other side view shows the length of the planter and the distribution of flowers and leaves, mirroring the opposite side.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged radially around a central disc, forming a daisy-like composite structure with clear symmetry and slight overlapping at the base.
